notes:
EX Deoxys (ex8), 2005 EX-era set. Booster = 9 cards / 36-packs per box, no ETB (vintage). Pack structure (Bulbapedia: 9-card EX-era packs; era baseline): 4 common + 3 uncommon + 1 reverse-holo filler + 1 rare-or-better slot = 9.

RARE SLOT distribution from ThePriceDex per-pack odds (rare 1/1.5=0.667, rare-holo 1/4=0.25, Pokemon-ex 1/13.5=0.074) plus Gold Star at the community-consensus 1/72 (~1 per 2 boxes, corroborated by Elite Fourum thread citing Flipside Gaming/PokeGym/smpratte). Note ThePriceDex lists Gold Star lower at 1/108; I used the better-sourced 1/72 community figure. These figures naturally sum to ~1.0 across the rare slot, confirming they are the rare-slot resolution distribution. Secret-rare (1 card in set) is not separately published for EX era and is rarer than Gold Star; carved a small 0.0021 (~1 in ~480) slice and normalized rare bucket so the slot sums to exactly 1.0.

REVERSE SLOT distribution from ThePriceDex reverse-rarity odds (RevCommon 1/2.3=0.435, RevUncommon 1/3.2=0.313, RevRare 1/7.9=0.127, RevRareHolo 1/7.3=0.137), mapped onto this set's buckets (common/uncommon/rare/rare-holo) and rounded to sum 1.0.

Confidence MEDIUM: ThePriceDex gives a per-set EX Deoxys table and the Gold Star rate has multi-source community corroboration, but no large published study exists for EX-era and secret-rare odds are inferred. EX Deoxys was printed in low quantities (one of the scarcest EX sets), so sealed boxes carry a large collector premium — EV/booster will sit well below box price, which is expected for vintage.

Available buckets used (all subset of given set): common, uncommon, rare, rare-holo, ex, gold-star, secret-rare.
